other_observers: Dan Maynard, Brandon Percival, Mark Peterson, others; Dan Maynard found this bird while we were birding for the Penrose CBC. He was about 50 feet from me on the other side of the narrow stream.
specific_location: At the top end of a very small open water stream that runs through a former dairy on Willow Road above the Arkansas River (south of it).
description: Empidonax by size and eyering. The bird was short-billed with bill also being quite narrow. I made sure to look closely at the bill. The head was gray and contrasting with the back and belly. The wings appeared long, and there was a noticeable longish primary projection. The tail was longish, not short like in Least Flycatcher. The breast was yellowish.Behaviors: Foraged low along small spring, flycatching sometimes and also catching some food items on the ground within a few feet of the bank.Call: None heardPlumage: basic
